title: Ranunculus porrectilobus
taxonomy: Ranunculus porrectilobus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It belongs to the genus Ranunculus and is native to South Africa. The plant is part of the Ranunculales order and is a member of the Ranunculales clade. Ranunculus porrectilobus is a type of buttercup, specifically a bulbous perennial. Its classification is further supported by its placement within the plant kingdom as a eudicot, also known as an angiosperm.

intro: Ranunculus porrectilobus, also known as the round-leaved buttercup, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus porrectilobus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
